data.title: China Mobile Books 15.73 Billion Yuan Asset Impairment Provision for 2024
data.description:
China Mobile's board approved a provision for asset impairment totaling 15.73 billion yuan for 2024 during a recent meeting, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The impairment includes 14.51 billion yuan for credit losses on accounts and other receivables and 1.22 billion yuan for other assets, such as inventory and fixed assets.

data.title: Ports deal puts China’s investment case to test
data.description:
Beijing’s displeasure with CK Hutchison’s $23 bln sale of its docks business to BlackRock comes just as overseas money managers' confidence in the People's Republic was returning. Defending geopolitical interests while wooing foreign capital will require a delicate balancing act.

Full view will be published shortly.

CONTEXT NEWS

The annual China Development Forum will take place on March 23 and 24 in Beijing. China President Xi Jinping is scheduled to meet with dozens of foreign CEOs including HSBC's Georges Elhedery and Blackstone's Stephen Schwarzman, Reuters reported on March 18, citing a source familiar with the matter.

CK Hutchison on March 20 reported an 11% drop in underlying profit for 2024 to $2.68 billion. Having cancelled all post-earnings conferences, Chair Victor Li said in a statement there might be headwinds with supply chain disruptions in the early part of 2025, partly due to “ongoing geopolitical risk impacting global trade”. The company on March 4 said it had agreed in principle to sell its 80% stake in its ports business, including operations along the Panama Canal, to a consortium led by BlackRock for $22.8 billion including debt.

data.title: Switching off plants: Chinese copper smelters grapple with margin collapse
data.description:
Key points:
  • Chinese smelters face overcapacity, tightening feedstock
  • Smelters may discuss output cuts on March 31, sources say
  • Treatment charges for copper concentrate hit record lows

Major copper smelters across top consumer China have kicked off equipment maintenance in March, traditionally a peak demand period, in a bid to stem losses from a worsening feedstock shortage that is hammering margins, industry insiders said.

Shutting down plants in what is traditionally one of the busiest times of the year highlights how much pain the scarcity of copper concentrate is causing refiners, a problem compounded by smelting overcapacity that has sparked fierce competition for the raw material and sent treatment fees below zero.

Roughly 980,000 metric tons of smelting capacity, or 8% of China's total last year, is set for maintenance in March, Hongyuan Futures said in a note. Three analysts and a smelter manager told Reuters this was an unusually high amount compared to previous years.

Staff at three large smelters confirmed to Reuters they began equipment maintenance in March. Another operator, Tongling Nonferrous Metals Group , which accounted for 13.5% of China's refined copper output in 2023, started month-long maintenance on some smelting equipment in early March, according to two sources.

Production cuts can be sensitive in China, where many smelters are important contributors to regional economies, but as in many industries, from steel to oil refining, the sector is plagued by overcapacity as operators chase market share.

The overcapacity in China, the world's largest supplier of the refined metal used in wiring, machinery and new energy technologies, is contributing to a global imbalance in the copper smelting industry.

By shutting equipment for maintenance, smelters hope to cut consumption of copper concentrate, ease the concentrate shortfall and stop processing fees from sliding even further, analysts said.

Eight analysts and smelters who spoke to did so on condition of anonymity given they are not authorised to speak to media.

Tongling did not respond to Reuters' request for comment.

The industry's difficulties put a spotlight on the March 31 quarterly meeting of the China Smelters Purchase Team (CSPT), where measures including production cuts are set to be discussed, sources with knowledge of the matter said.

"There is a significant risk of large-scale output cuts among Chinese smelters this year," said Patricia Barreto, senior analyst at S&P Global Commodity Insights.

If Chinese smelters jointly agree a production cut, refined copper output this year will likely fall, which could lead China to import more, analysts said.

UNUSUAL TIMING

Widespread maintenance is unusual in March, when smelters historically benefit from a surge in demand after the Lunar New Year holiday before undergoing maintenance in April or May.

Supply of copper concentrate has been tight since late 2023 due to disruptions at major mines including First Quantum's Cobre mine in Panama as well as smelter capacity expansion.

That overcapacity, which continues to grow, is increasing demand for globally scarce concentrate.

Analysts at Antaike and Shanghai Metals Market have lowered forecasts for copper concentrate output growth this year due to downward revisions from producers, according to research notes from Citi.

Some smelters are operating at a loss, and not just in China.

Glencore's Pasar smelter in the Philippines was put on care and maintenance in February. Japan's smelters, while bunkering down for tough times, may fare better as they are more diversified than China's and have long-term supply contracts.

Treatment and refining charges (TC/RCs), a key revenue source, have been negative since December 20, meaning smelters must pay miners or traders to turn concentrate into refined metal, instead of the other way around.

Fastmarkets copper concentrates TC/RC index plunged to a record low of -$26.5 per ton and -2.65 cents per pound on March 7.

"The situation is much tougher this year, as last year smelters could make some money thanks to better prices for the long-term contract, but you are suffering losses in whatever situation right now," said a manager at a Chinese smelter.

"The only difference is whether you are losing more money or less money," the manager added, saying losses were as much as 3,000 yuan ($414.47) per ton of refined copper for spot cargoes.

Four analysts estimated losses at between 1,000 yuan and 2,000 yuan per ton.

data.title: Regulator Starts Investigation over Potential Concerns at OD6 Metals' New South Wales Project
data.description:
OD6 Metals said that the New South Wales Resources Regulator has initiated an investigation into potential Mining Act violations related to its exploration license EL 8492 at the Gulf Creek Copper Project, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The investigation follows the regulator's March 5 inspection of the project, which raised environmental compliance concerns, including groundwater management, surface water controls, and site disturbance issues, the filing said.

OD6 suspended exploration at the project on March 11 after corrective measures were deemed insufficient, the filing added.

The company is cooperating with the regulator and assessing the investigation's impact.

data.title: Tianan Coal Mining Sets 4.15 Billion Yuan For This Year's Production, Operations
data.description:
Pingdingshan Tianan Coal Mining said that its production and operational investment plan for the year is estimated at 4.15 billion yuan, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

About 1.87 billion yuan will be spent on basic mine construction, production area replacement and system optimization, while an investment of 2.17 billion yuan is planned for fixed asset renovation and 102 million yuan is set for maintaining simple reproduction.

In the previous year, the Chinese coal miner spent 4.70 billion yuan for its production and operations, lower than the intended 5.35 billion yuan that was set for the operations, the disclosure said.

The company's shares fell less than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Singapore Dollar Slightly Weakens Ahead of 'Triple Witching Day' — Market Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar weakens slightly against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session as traders brace for a 'triple witching day,' Maybank analysts say, pointing to when the expiry of equity index futures, equity index options and equity options coincide, the analysts say in a FX Research & Strategy report. "This sets the stage for heightened volatility in equities that could also spillover to currencies," the analysts say. The 'triple witching day' historically tends to have a bearish bias for equities, which could offer support for safe-havens like the USD, the analysts add. USD/SGD edges 0.1% higher to 1.3358. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Hanwha Aerospace Shares Tumble After Share-Sale Announcement
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui and Jihye Lee

Hanwha Aerospace's shares tumbled Friday after the company unveiled a plan to raise 3.6 trillion won, equivalent to $2.45 billion, via a share sale.

Shares of the weapons maker plunged as much as 16% to 608,000 won, the lowest intraday level since Feb. 28, according to FactSet. Shares were last quoted 13% down at 631,000 won.

The South Korean defense company plans to issue 5.95 million common shares at 605,000 won per share, it said in a statement after market close Thursday. That's around 16% below Thursday's market closing price.

The company said it expects proceeds from the offering to be approximately 3.6 trillion won. It plans to use the proceeds to invest in its own facilities and acquire stakes in other companies to secure overseas production capabilities, including investing in foreign defense joint ventures and shipbuilders.

Hanwha Aerospace plans to allocate 600 billion won of the proceeds in building a joint venture with a partner in Europe and the Middle East to expand in the region.

This is the largest ever rights offering capital raise in Korea, said Douglas Kim, analyst at Douglas Research Advisory, who publishes on Smartkarma, in commentary.

"This capital raise is likely to result in higher concerns about shares dilution at Hanwha Aerospace," Kim added.

Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com and Jihye Lee at jihye.lee@wsj.com

data.title: China's yuan slips to one-week low, looks set for weekly drop
data.description:
China's yuan slipped to a one-week low against the dollar on Friday and looked set for a losing week, reflecting a rebound in greenback in overseas markets, while the central bank continued to pare back support through its official daily fixing.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's comments signalling no rush to cut interest rates in the United States supported the greenback and pressured emerging market currencies.

Fears of a possible escalation in trade disputes between the world's two largest economies in coming weeks also remained a drag on the yuan sentiment, traders said.

Meanwhile, the central bank appeared to dial back its support for the Chinese currency via its daily midpoint fixings, with a months-long gap between the official guidance and market projections narrowing. Market participants interpreted that as a sign policymakers are looking to prevent any fast yuan appreciation in a benign dollar environment.

Prior to the market opening on Friday, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) set the midpoint rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at 7.1760 per dollar, its weakest since January 20 and 663 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate of 7.2423.

Analysts and traders usually use the gap between official fixing and market projections to gauge the official foreign exchange stance. The gap soared to a much as 1,557 pips in mid-January and has been wider than 1,000 pips for most of the year to date. A wider gap suggested stronger support to keep the yuan steady.

The onshore yuan eased to a low of 7.2524 per dollar in morning deals, the weakest level since March 14, before changing hands at 7.2484 at 0300 GMT, while its offshore counterpart traded at 7.2530.

Developments in trade relations with the United States continue to remain front and centre on investors' radar. President Donald Trump still intends to impose new reciprocal tariffs on a number of U.S. trading partners on April 2, the White House said this week.

"It is likely that more tariffs or other actions could be announced after the U.S. administration finishes its various reviews on China's trade," analysts at HSBC said in a note.

"Tariff-induced depreciation pressure on the RMB is, therefore, still very high. Even though we believe the Chinese authorities have their own motivations for keeping USD/RMB reasonably stable, some moderate adjustment may still be warranted later to alleviate the impact on exporters."

They retained their view that the yuan will trade at 7.4 per dollar at the end of this year.

"The yuan exchange rate could be faced with near-term depreciation pressure with additional tariffs effective," Citi analysts said in a note.

"A (political) deal, if it rolls back additional tariffs, could by nature lead to yuan appreciation, We also expect FX discussions to be included in the potential deal, implicitly or explicitly."

Citi expects the yuan to hit 7.4 in three months before pulling back to 7.3 in six to 12 months.

data.title: Thailand Export Growth Hits 4-Month High
data.description:
Exports from Thailand soared 14% year-on-year in February 2025, following a 13.6% surge in the previous month.

This marked the eighth consecutive month of export expansion and the fastest pace since last October.

In 2024, exports grew 5.4% to USD 300.5 billion, compared to a 1% decline in 2023.

data.title: Market Chatter: Samsung Electronics to Unveil AI-Driven Smart Home Appliances
data.description:
Samsung Electronics will introduce its latest AI-powered home appliances and unveil its vision for hyper-personalized smart living at the Welcome to Bespoke AI event on March 26.

The company will unveil Home AI, an advanced ecosystem that enhances connectivity and smart home automation, Yonhap News reported Thursday.

Samsung will also launch its latest Bespoke AI lineup, featuring customizable appliances that can be altered to requirements for South Korean consumers, it said.

Shares of Samsung Electronics rose nearly 2% in recent trade on Friday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Ringgit May Trade in 4.42-4.45 Range Versus Dollar Next Week — Market Talk
data.description: The Malaysian ringgit could remain range bound next week, trading between 4.42 and 4.45 against the U.S. dollar, as the risk of reciprocal tariffs in early April keeps market sentiment cautious, Kenanga IB economists say in a note. However, domestic economic optimism, along with China's improving outlook and Europe's resilience, should help limit the ringgit's downside, they reckon. Technically, the pair's outlook remains neutral and could hover around 4.432, they add. Kenanga pegs USD/MYR's short-term resistance at 4.442 and support at 4.419. USD/MYR is 0.1% higher at 4.422. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

Camu Camu isn’t just another exotic berry—it’s one of the most powerful natural metabolism boosters on the planet. Loaded with vitamin C and antioxidants, it plays a pivotal role in fat oxidation, ensuring your body efficiently converts stored fat into usable energy.

  • Boosts thermogenesis: This means your body burns more calories—even at rest—by slightly increasing its internal heat production.
  • Prevents new fat accumulation: EGCG inhibits the enzymes responsible for storing fat, making it harder for your body to cling onto excess weight.
  • Enhances liver function for fat metabolism: The liver plays a huge role in processing fat, and EGCG ensures it’s working at peak efficiency.

Many products claim to include green tea extract, but they fail to standardize the EGCG content—meaning the amount of active fat-burning compounds is too low to have a real impact. Nagano Tonic fixes this by using a pure, concentrated form of EGCG that delivers maximum metabolic activation.

>> See why Nagano Tonic’s EGCG blend is superior to generic fat burners – Click here.

3. Panax Ginseng – The Ancient Metabolism Enhancer That Supports Gut Health

Panax Ginseng has been used for centuries in Eastern medicine, but modern research confirms that its weight loss benefits are backed by science. In Nagano Tonic, this adaptogenic herb serves a dual purpose:

  • Stimulates metabolism by improving gut bacteria balance – Studies show that overweight individuals have an imbalance of gut bacteria that makes weight loss harder. Ginseng reverses this, creating an environment where your body naturally burns fat more efficiently.
  • Prevents stress-induced weight gain – Cortisol spikes due to stress cause fat storage around the belly. Panax Ginseng regulates cortisol levels, making it easier to shed stubborn fat.
  • Boosts overall energy and endurance – Unlike caffeine, which causes a sudden spike and crash, ginseng provides steady, long-lasting energy.

Nagano Tonic’s ginseng content isn’t just thrown in for show—it’s included in a scientifically optimal dose that ensures you actually experience its metabolism-revving benefits.

>> See why Panax Ginseng is a must-have for fat loss – Learn more here.

4. Momordica Charantia – The Hidden Blood Sugar Regulator That Stops Fat Storage

Also known as bitter melon, Momordica Charantia is a powerful weight management ingredient that works by keeping blood sugar levels in check.

Why is this crucial for fat loss?

  • Stable blood sugar prevents fat storage – Every time you eat, your body releases insulin. High insulin spikes = more fat storage. Bitter melon prevents unnecessary insulin surges, making it easier for your body to burn fat instead of store it.
  • Increases carbohydrate metabolism – Instead of storing carbs as fat, your body uses them for energy, helping you stay active and burn more calories throughout the day.
  • Supports healthy digestion and gut balance – A sluggish gut leads to a sluggish metabolism. Momordica Charantia helps regulate digestion, ensuring your metabolism functions at peak capacity.

Nagano Tonic ensures the correct concentration of bitter melon extract, so you reap the benefits without worrying about bitterness or unpleasant side effects.

>> Discover how Momordica Charantia supercharges Nagano Tonic’s weight loss power – Click here.

5. Cinnamon Cassia – The Thermogenic Powerhouse That Activates Fat-Burning Mode

Cinnamon Cassia isn’t just a flavorful spice—it’s a proven metabolic booster that enhances insulin sensitivity and thermogenesis.

Here’s why it’s essential in Nagano Tonic:

  • Forces the body to burn more calories after eating – Cinnamon increases post-meal energy expenditure, helping you burn calories rather than store them.
  • Regulates insulin for steady energy – No more energy crashes caused by wild blood sugar fluctuations.
  • Amplifies other fat-burning ingredients – Cinnamon works synergistically with Camu Camu and EGCG, enhancing their effects for greater weight loss results.

Most people don’t realize that standard store-bought cinnamon lacks the necessary compounds to promote weight loss. Nagano Tonic uses a highly concentrated form of Cinnamon Cassia, ensuring every sip contributes to real metabolic activation.

>> Find out why Cinnamon Cassia is a must-have for weight loss – Click here.

Why Nagano Tonic’s Exact Formula Matters More Than Just the Ingredients

Most supplements fail to deliver results because they include random ingredients in ineffective dosages. Nagano Tonic is different—each ingredient is precisely measured and clinically tested to work in harmony.

This carefully crafted balance ensures:

  • Each ingredient amplifies the effects of the others, making the formula more powerful than taking the ingredients individually.
  • Optimal absorption and bioavailability, meaning your body actually uses the nutrients rather than flushing them out.
  • No unnecessary additives or fillers—just pure, metabolism-enhancing compounds that work.

This isn’t something you can replicate at home by throwing ingredients together in a blender. The precision of Nagano Tonic’s formulation is what makes it a true fat-burning powerhouse.

>> Experience the science-backed power of Nagano Tonic – Order today.

Is Nagano Tonic Safe? Side Effects, Medical Backing & FDA Compliance

When it comes to supplements, safety is just as important as effectiveness. Many people hesitate before trying a new weight management formula, wondering if it could lead to unexpected side effects, long-term health risks, or hidden harmful ingredients. Nagano Tonic stands out as a meticulously formulated, thoroughly tested product that prioritizes safety just as much as results.

To put your mind at ease, let’s break down the medical backing, ingredient safety, manufacturing standards, and real-world side effect reports to determine whether Nagano Tonic is truly a risk-free addition to your weight loss routine.

Every Ingredient in Nagano Tonic Is Scientifically Tested for Safety

Nagano Tonic isn’t a random mix of trendy ingredients—it’s a scientifically developed formula where every component is chosen based on extensive clinical research. Each ingredient has been studied for both its weight loss benefits and its safety profile, ensuring it doesn’t cause harm even with long-term use.

For instance, Camu Camu, a key metabolism-boosting ingredient in Nagano Tonic, has been extensively researched for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Studies show that high doses of Camu Camu do not pose any toxicity risks, making it a safe addition to daily supplementation.

Similarly, EGCG from green tea has been studied for its fat-burning and thermogenic properties, but also for its long-term effects on liver health. High doses of low-quality green tea extracts have been linked to liver stress in certain supplements, but Nagano Tonic only includes EGCG in clinically approved, non-toxic amounts.

Additionally, Momordica Charantia (bitter melon) and Panax Ginseng are widely used in traditional medicine, with hundreds of years of human consumption proving their safety. Scientific reviews confirm that they support metabolism and energy levels without causing dependency, jitters, or withdrawal symptoms.

One major concern with weight loss products is stimulant overload—many brands rely on synthetic caffeine and dangerous fat-burning stimulants to create rapid results, leading to heart palpitations, anxiety, and blood pressure spikes. Nagano Tonic is completely free from artificial stimulants, making it a far safer choice than conventional diet pills.

>> Learn more about Nagano Tonic’s medically tested ingredients – Click here.

GMP Certification & FDA Compliance Ensure Nagano Tonic’s 100% Safety

Not all supplements are created under the same conditions. While some products cut corners in manufacturing, leading to inconsistent potency and potential contamination, Nagano Tonic is produced in FDA-approved facilities that adhere to the strictest safety protocols.

The formula is manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), which means every batch undergoes rigorous quality control. This ensures:

  • Each bottle contains exactly what’s listed on the label—no hidden ingredients or filler compounds.
  • Contaminants like heavy metals, pesticides, and harmful bacteria are strictly tested against—guaranteeing product purity.
  • Ingredients are sourced responsibly and meet pharmaceutical-grade standards, ensuring potency without risk.

FDA compliance also means that Nagano Tonic adheres to strict safety guidelines for dietary supplements, preventing substandard or dangerous formulations from reaching consumers. Unlike many weight loss products that skirt regulatory requirements, Nagano Tonic maintains full transparency in its sourcing, production, and testing methods.

Consumers can trust that every scoop of Nagano Tonic is safe, consistent, and formulated under the highest industry standards—not made in unregulated overseas facilities with unknown ingredient sourcing.

Debunking Myths About Natural Ingredients Causing Harm

A common misconception is that "natural" ingredients can still be harmful, leading to skepticism around herbal-based supplements. While it’s true that not all natural ingredients are safe, the ones in Nagano Tonic have been individually tested for both effectiveness and safety.

For example, some people believe that green tea extract (EGCG) can damage the liver—but the reality is that problems only occur with excessive synthetic extracts, not naturally derived, properly dosed EGCG like the one in Nagano Tonic. Clinical studies confirm that EGCG in moderate amounts actually supports liver function, enhances detoxification, and protects against oxidative stress.

Similarly, some mistakenly assume that bitter melon (Momordica Charantia) causes digestive distress, but this only occurs in extremely high doses or when consumed raw in large quantities. In Nagano Tonic, it is formulated in a way that maximizes benefits without causing discomfort, helping to balance blood sugar and improve metabolic function safely.

Another myth is that Panax Ginseng and Cinnamon Cassia overstimulate metabolism and cause rapid heartbeat. In reality, these ingredients support natural thermogenesis without affecting cardiovascular health. Many weight loss supplements overload users with caffeine and artificial stimulants, creating a false sense of increased metabolism—Nagano Tonic avoids these risky tactics altogether.

These concerns often arise because low-quality, unregulated weight loss supplements misuse ingredients—but Nagano Tonic’s carefully controlled dosages eliminate these risks, making it a safe, non-toxic choice for daily use.

>> Find out how Nagano Tonic ensures safety without compromising effectiveness – Click here.

Long-Term Safety: No Risks After Months of Use

One of the biggest dangers with weight loss supplements is their long-term impact on metabolism, digestion, and energy levels. Many products work for a few weeks, only to cause crashes, withdrawal symptoms, or metabolic slowdowns later on.

Nagano Tonic is specifically designed to be safe for extended use, with no risk of dependency, adaptation, or diminishing results. Here’s why:

  • It does not contain synthetic stimulants that cause withdrawal effects when stopped.
  • The metabolism-enhancing properties are gradual and sustainable, avoiding the "burnout effect" that many fat burners create.
  • It supports gut health rather than disrupting digestion, making it beneficial even after months of consistent use.

People using Nagano Tonic for 6+ months report continuous benefits, with no negative side effects or metabolic crashes—a rarity in the supplement industry. This is because the formula is built to work with the body’s natural processes, not against them.

No Reports of Serious Side Effects – Consumers Trust Nagano Tonic’s Purity

The real test of a supplement’s safety isn’t just its formulation—it’s what actual users report after trying it. With thousands of satisfied customers, Nagano Tonic has no history of severe side effects, allergic reactions, or health risks.

While some first-time users may experience minor digestive adjustments—which is common when introducing metabolism-supporting nutrients—these effects are temporary and subside as the body adapts. Unlike other weight loss formulas that cause nausea, headaches, or jitters, Nagano Tonic is gentle on the system.

One major reason for its positive safety record is that it is free from artificial preservatives, synthetic stimulants, or chemically altered compounds. Every ingredient is sourced from nature and extensively tested for purity, ensuring that users only receive the cleanest, most effective version of each nutrient.

Customers consistently report that Nagano Tonic not only supports weight loss but also enhances energy, mental clarity, and digestion without any unwanted side effects. This is why Nagano Tonic continues to be a trusted choice for those looking for a safe, natural weight management solution.

>> Avoid risky diet pills – Order Nagano Tonic from the official site for guaranteed purity.

Why Nagano Tonic Is Disrupting the Weight Loss Industry – What Makes It So Special?

Weight loss supplements have existed for decades, but most rely on stimulants, appetite suppressants, or crash dieting principles that do more harm than good. Nagano Tonic has taken a completely different approach, targeting the root cause of slow metabolism rather than just forcing short-term weight loss.

Instead of using harsh chemicals or synthetic fat burners, Nagano Tonic taps into metabolic activation through natural, clinically-backed ingredients. Camu Camu, EGCG, and Momordica Charantia work in unison to fire up a dormant metabolism—a factor that many weight loss products fail to address.

Another game-changing factor is that Nagano Tonic doesn’t rely on caffeine or stimulants to create the illusion of fat burning. Traditional weight loss pills flood your system with high doses of caffeine, leading to temporary energy surges but inevitable crashes. Nagano Tonic, on the other hand, supports sustainable energy levels without forcing the body into a stress state.

Because of this breakthrough formulation, Nagano Tonic has been creating massive buzz in the industry, attracting users who have tried everything else and failed. It’s no longer about quick fixes—people want safe, effective, and long-term results, and that’s why Nagano Tonic is standing out.

>> Discover why Nagano Tonic is changing the weight loss industry – Click here.

The Hidden Dangers of Conventional Weight Loss Methods – Why Nagano Tonic Is a Safer Choice

Most people struggling with weight loss have already tried restrictive diets, fat-burning pills, and high-intensity exercise regimens. But the problem? These methods often backfire, creating more harm than good.

  • Starvation diets slow down metabolism—When calorie intake is cut too drastically, the body enters survival mode, storing more fat instead of burning it.
  • Caffeine-based fat burners cause adrenal fatigue—While caffeine may increase metabolism in the short term, overuse leads to hormonal imbalances, anxiety, and exhaustion.
  • Prescription weight loss drugs come with serious risks—Many pharmaceutical weight loss pills have been linked to heart problems, liver toxicity, and dangerous side effects.

Nagano Tonic offers a radically different approach—instead of forcing the body into weight loss through starvation or stimulants, it restores natural metabolic function. The carefully curated blend of ingredients like Panax Ginseng and Inulin works with your body’s natural processes rather than against them.

Unlike conventional methods that leave users feeling drained and frustrated, Nagano Tonic enhances energy, digestion, and fat-burning without negative consequences.

Real Users vs. Fake Reviews – What Are People Actually Saying About Nagano Tonic?

Whenever a supplement starts gaining traction, competitors begin trying to sabotage its reputation—this is exactly what’s happening with Nagano Tonic.

While thousands of satisfied users have reported significant weight loss, increased energy, and reduced cravings, a handful of misleading negative reviews have surfaced, often coming from competing brands attempting to discredit the product.

What Real Users Are Saying About Nagano Tonic

  • Claire S. – Dayton, OH: "After struggling with my weight for years, I gave Nagano Tonic a shot. I’ve lost 18 lbs in 8 weeks, and my cravings are finally under control!"
  • Mike C. – Ann Arbor, MI: "I noticed an immediate change in my energy levels after starting Nagano Tonic. I feel great throughout the day, and my metabolism has definitely improved."
  • Hazel W. – Norfolk, VA: "I was skeptical at first, but after 2 months of using Nagano Tonic, I’m blown away by my progress. My clothes fit better, and my digestion has improved significantly."

With over 50,000+ satisfied users, Nagano Tonic has proven to be a legitimate, effective weight loss formula, despite what some biased reviews may claim.

Conclusion – Is Nagano Tonic Worth Trying?

After analyzing its formulation, safety, user reviews, and clinical backing, the answer is clear—Nagano Tonic is one of the most promising weight loss supplements on the market today. Unlike synthetic fat burners that cause crashes and health risks, Nagano Tonic takes a sustainable, science-backed approach to boosting metabolism, reducing cravings, and enhancing energy.

With thousands of positive reviews, a 180-day money-back guarantee, and transparent ingredient sourcing, Nagano Tonic stands out as a trusted solution for anyone looking to break free from weight loss struggles.

For those serious about achieving real, lasting results, securing a 3- or 6-bottle supply directly from the official website is the best way to get started.

data.title: Financial Times - March 21
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Financial Times. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

Headlines

- London's Heathrow airport to be closed all day after fire cuts power

- GB Energy makes first investment with 110 million pounds for clean energy

- European military powers work on 5-10 year plan to replace US in Nato

- Accenture warns Elon Musk's Doge-led spending crackdown is hitting revenues

Overview

- London's Heathrow airport has been forced to close until midnight after a fire at a nearby electricity substation caused a "significant power outage".

- The first investment for the UK's new state-owned energy company Great British Energy will be 110 million pounds in grants to fund solar panels and other clean energy production.

- Europe's biggest military powers are drawing up plans to take on greater responsibilities for the continent's defence from the US, including a pitch to the Trump administration for a managed transfer over the next five to 10 years.

- Accenture has warned that Elon Musk's efforts to slash U.S. government spending have started to affect its revenues, with the consulting group also highlighting threats from global economic uncertainty.

data.title: Get Nice Secures Court Approval to Acquire, Delist Nice Financial
data.description:
Get Nice Holdings (GNH) secured court approval in the Cayman Islands to acquire the outstanding shares of Nice Financial Group (GNF), according to a filing with the Hong Kong bourse on Friday.

The reorganization involves a share exchange offer where GNH will acquire all GNF shares. This will be accompanied by a scheme dividend distribution to GNF shareholders, with payments set for April 3.

Following the reorganization, GNF shares will be delisted from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange at the end of trading day on March 27.

The first day of dealings of the new GNH Shares issued to the scheme shareholders is expected to be on April 7.

Get Nice's shares were down 4% in recent trading.

data.title: Xref CFO Resigns, Initiates Restructuring, Shares Up 8%
data.description:
Xref said its Chief Financial Officer James Solomon has decided to resign amid an ongoing restructuring process, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The company's Finance Director will assume the role of CFO while keeping his current role, the filing added.

The company said its management has recently initiated a restructuring to deliver annual cost savings of about AU$900,000.

The restructuring changes will not impact revenue growth or product development but will enhance operating cash flow through reduced overheads, the filing said.

The company's shares were up 8% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Thailand Posts Largest Trade Surplus in Near 1-1/2-Years
data.description:
Thailand posted a trade surplus of USD 2 billion in February 2025, shifting from a USD 0.55 billion deficit in the same month of the previous year.

It marked the first trade surplus in five months and the largest since September 2023, mainly due to a surge in exports.

Last year, the trade balance registered a deficit of USD 6.28 billion, with exports rising by 5.4%, less than the 6.3% growth in imports.

data.title: Role of Indonesia central bank must be strengthened to support growth ambitions, top lawmaker says
data.description:
The role of the Indonesia's central bank must be strengthened to help support the government's economic growth target of 8% by 2029, a lawmaker heading a parliamentary committee overseeing the financial sector said on Friday.

Mukhamad Misbakhun said the commission has been preparing measures on how parliament could help the government to achieve its 8% target from the current 5%, including strengthening the roles of the finance ministry as well as Bank Indonesia.

"If our economic growth is driven by two engines of fiscal and monetary policy, this will be a strong national combination going forward," he said at a capital markets forum in Jakarta.

He did not elaborate on how the central bank's role could be enhanced but said he had met BI officials the previous night.

Indonesia's economic growth has hovered at around 5% in the past few years.

Parliament is currently discussing a revision of the 2023 Development and Strengthening of the Financial Sector Law, after the Constitutional Court ordered lawmakers earlier this year to review it on matters related to the independence of the state deposit insurer agency.

There have been concerns, however, that lawmakers might also reevaluate the central's bank role. Its Governor Perry Warjiyo has acknowledged there had been talks about the central bank's mandate in those parliamentary discussions, but no changes were expected.

BI's mandate is currently supporting sustainable economic growth on top of rupiah and price stability.

data.title: CGTN: China advances high-quality rural development in line with local conditions
data.description:
CGTN published an article on Chinese President Xi Jinping’s inspection tour in southwest China’s Yunnan Province. Through introducing Yunnan’s efforts in advancing the “flower economy” and fostering cultural tourism, the article highlights the province’s push for high-quality rural development in line with local conditions, aiming to break new ground in the pursuit of Chinese modernization.

BEIJING, March 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Located in Lijiang in southwest China's Yunnan Province, the Lijiang Modern Flower Industrial Park is an epitome of China's efforts to advance rural revitalization.

Empowered by intelligent production system, all the greenhouses in the park use soilless cultivation technology, with the coverage rate of integrated water and fertilizer irrigation facilities reaching more than 85 percent.

Relying on the nearby Lijiang Airport, products such as fresh-cut roses and calla lilies can be delivered by air to cities like Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou within 36 hours, and sold to countries such as Japan, Vietnam and Russia, creating jobs for more than 300 people in the surrounding rural areas.

Visiting the park on Wednesday afternoon, Chinese President Xi Jinping spoke with villagers and technicians, inquiring about flower varieties, market sales and their incomes.

The villagers told Xi that they earned over 4,000 yuan (about $557.46) per month on average, with their monthly incomes exceeding 7,000 yuan during peak seasons. Growing flowers is a truly joyful job, they shared.

"Your endeavor is thriving and aligns with the development path of modern agriculture. May your lives be as beautiful as flowers," Xi said with delight.

'Flower economy'

Developing distinctive industries has always been Xi's focus of attention during his domestic inspections in recent years. "The key to rural revitalization is industrial revitalization," he once said.

Yunnan's unique geographical and climatic conditions have earned the province the reputation of the "plant kingdom," giving rise to the development of "flower economy." For many years, Yunnan's output of fresh-cut flowers has ranked top in the country.

In 2024, the province's flower planting area reached 1.95 million mu (about 130,000 hectares), including 350,000 mu dedicated to fresh-cut flowers. A total of 20.6 billion fresh-cut flowers were produced, ranking first globally.

The flower industry in Yunnan directly employs 380,000 people in planting, sorting and packaging, logistics and e-commerce, with over 1 million more employed indirectly. Decades of efforts by local people have established a full-fledged industrial chain, including Asia's first and the world's second-largest flower auction center.

At the Kunming International Flora Auction Trading Center, the average daily trading volume reached 7.06 million stems in 2024, with an average transaction time of only 4 seconds per order and a transaction rate of 95.34 percent.

Flowers auctioned here are not only sent to major Chinese cities, but also exported to over 50 countries and regions.

Promoting industrial transformation and upgrade is the key task of high-quality development, Xi emphasized during the inspection, calling on Yunnan to actively foster strategic emerging industries and future industries.

Integration of culture and tourism

Xi also visited the Old Town of Lijiang on Wednesday to learn about local efforts to enhance the protection and utilization of historical and cultural heritage.

"The town's culture, scenery and folk traditions are truly captivating," Xi said, adding that the integration of culture and tourism has boosted the economy.

The Old Town of Lijiang, with a history of more than 800 years, was listed as a world cultural heritage site by the UNESCO in 1997. Over the past 27 years, the town has built 30 cultural courtyards, creating new scenarios for cultural heritage and promoting the development of the local cultural and tourism industry.

Today, the town's tourism industry continues to thrive, with business spanning catering, accommodation, handicrafts and performing arts. An increasing number of young people are starting businesses here, committing themselves to the inheritance and promotion of national cultural heritage.

Currently, the town is vigorously developing cultural heritage tourism by expanding cultural attractions and street landmarks. By organizing various activities to create a rich cultural atmosphere, Lijiang successfully attracts visitors from all over the world.

From January to September last year, the town welcomed a total of 17.05 million tourists, a year-on-year increase of 13.07 percent.

It is imperative to promote cultural tourism to improve people's income and properly handle the relationship between protection and development, Xi said, urging that the cultural and tourism industry should follow a path of sustainable and healthy development.

For more information, please click:

https://news.cgtn.com/news/2025-03-20/China-advances-high-quality-rural-development-based-on-local-condition-1BTyBnQ4Bd6/p.html

Contact: CGTN

Email: cgtn@cgtn.com

data.title: US Senator Steve Daines will visit China for trade talks
data.description:
Senator Steve Daines, a Trump ally, will visit China for trade talks, marking the first high-level U.S. political meeting there since Trump’s return.

  • The trip will aim to revive stalled trade negotiations amid rising tariff tensions, with Daines promoting the “America First” agenda.
  • Analysts see the visit as a form of quiet diplomacy, as both countries seek to avoid further economic disruption.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.3% Lower at 76155.00
Yonghui Superstores' Reform Leadership Team Temporarily Takes Over Chief Executive Officer Post; Shares Down 4%
data.description:
Yonghui Superstores' board of directors authorized the company's reform leadership team to temporarily perform the duties of a chief executive officer, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

The team will be in the CEO post to ensure the company's proper operation until a new CEO is appointed, the Chinese supermarket operator said.

The company's shares dropped less than 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Heatec Jietong Sets Up JV in Singapore with TEMA India
data.description:
Heatec Jietong Holdings subsidiary Heatec Jietong Pte. Ltd. entered into a joint venture agreement with TEMA India to establish a joint venture company in Singapore dubbed Tema Heatec, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The new entity will be involved in the promotion and supply of process equipment, skids modules and other fabricated items.

Both parties have agreed on an initial funding of SG$20,000 in the JV company. Heatec Jietong will provide SG$9,800 for a 49% stake, while TEMA India will contribute SG$10,200 for a 51% shareholding.

data.title: High Peak Royalties to Sell South Australia Unit to Natural Resources Company
data.description:
High Peak Royalties agreed to sell its subsidiary Torrens Energy (SA), which holds its South Australian geothermal assets, to natural resources firm Northstar Energy, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Consideration for the transaction includes the assumption by Northstar Energy of a royalty issued by Torrens Energy to the company and the issuance of 12.5 million completion shares in Northstar priced at AU$0.16 apiece, at a valuation of AU$2 million, subject to a two-tranche completion, the filing said.

The company also formed a formal partnership with Northstar Energy, committing to work with them and potentially participating in capital raising initiatives in the event of an initial public offering or similar liquidity event, per the filing.

data.title: Agri Picks Report March 21, 2025: Geojit Financial Services
data.description:
Geojit Financial Services's report on Daily Agri Picks

The sowing of summer crops in Maharashtra has reached 359,482 hectares as of Monday, up 29% on year from 278,687 hectares sown in the same period last year, according to a report released by the state agriculture department. Acreage under maize rose a whopping 101.7% on year to 66,022 hectares from 32,730 hectares last year. The acreage of paddy fell to 120,873 hectares from 130,648 hectares sown a year ago, the report showed. On the other hand, the area sown under bajra was 29,123 hectares, up from 22,522 hectares last year while jowar acreage also rose to 25,317 hectares from 18,402 hectares a year ago. According to the report, moong acreage so far rose to 7,103 hectares from 4,509 hectares last year and the area sown under urad increased to 1,196 hectares from 408 hectares a year ago. The total area sown under all oilseeds was 106,422 hectares, up from 67,433 hectares sown in the previous year, as per the report. Under oilseeds, the acreage of groundnut rose to 74,538 hectares from 54,068 hectares a year ago. The area sown under sesame rose to 23,646 hectares from 9,129 hectares last year. Summer crops are grown for a short period between the kharif and rabi seasons, with harvest taking place between April and May. Besides rice, sesame, and groundnut, the other major summer crops in the state include fruits and vegetables such as banana, pumpkin, and tomato. The water level in dams across Maharashtra was 51.2% of the live storage capacity as of Wednesday, compared with 42.5% a year ago, according to data from the state water resources department.

For all commodities report, click here

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts/broking houses/rating agencies on moneycontrol.com are their own, and not that of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

21032025 - co

data.title: Abundance International to Dispose of Stake in Shanghai Sunrise Polymer Material; Shares Surge 21%
data.description:
Abundance International subsidiary, Abundance Investments, has agreed to dispose of its entire 12.74% stake in Shanghai Sunrise Polymer Material to Changzhou Ju Ke Emerging Industry Venture Capital Fund Partnership Enterprise.

The consideration for the disposal amount to 62.4 million yuan, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Shares of the company surged over 21% in recent trading.

At the Kunming International Flora Auction Trading Center, the average daily trading volume reached 7.06 million stems in 2024, with an average transaction time of only 4 seconds per order and a transaction rate of 95.34 percent.

Flowers auctioned here are not only sent to major Chinese cities, but also exported to over 50 countries and regions.

Promoting industrial transformation and upgrade is the key task of high-quality development, Xi emphasized during the inspection, calling on Yunnan to actively foster strategic emerging industries and future industries.

Integration of culture and tourism

Xi also visited the Old Town of Lijiang on Wednesday to learn about local efforts to enhance the protection and utilization of historical and cultural heritage.

"The town's culture, scenery and folk traditions are truly captivating," Xi said, adding that the integration of culture and tourism has boosted the economy.

The Old Town of Lijiang, with a history of more than 800 years, was listed as a world cultural heritage site by the UNESCO in 1997. Over the past 27 years, the town has built 30 cultural courtyards, creating new scenarios for cultural heritage and promoting the development of the local cultural and tourism industry.

Today, the town's tourism industry continues to thrive, with business spanning catering, accommodation, handicrafts and performing arts. An increasing number of young people are starting businesses here, committing themselves to the inheritance and promotion of national cultural heritage.

Currently, the town is vigorously developing cultural heritage tourism by expanding cultural attractions and street landmarks. By organizing various activities to create a rich cultural atmosphere, Lijiang successfully attracts visitors from all over the world.

From January to September last year, the town welcomed a total of 17.05 million tourists, a year-on-year increase of 13.07 percent.

It is imperative to promote cultural tourism to improve people's income and properly handle the relationship between protection and development, Xi said, urging that the cultural and tourism industry should follow a path of sustainable and healthy development.

For more information, please click:

https://news.cgtn.com/news/2025-03-20/China-advances-high-quality-rural-development-based-on-local-condition-1BTyBnQ4Bd6/p.html

Contact: CGTN

Email: cgtn@cgtn.com

data.title: Cowell e Holdings' 2024 Profit Soars 156%; EPS, Revenue Beat Estimates; Shares Rise 10%
data.description:
Cowell e Holdings' attributable profit soared 156% to $119.1 million in 2024 from $46.6 million in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the optical products company increased to $0.134 from $0.053 a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS at $0.12.

Revenue surged 170% to $2.49 billion from $923.8 million in the previous year. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at $2.07 billion.

Shares jumped 10% during Friday's morning trading.

data.title: China's criticism of Hutchison ports deal raises stakes for TikTok US sale
data.description:
Key points:
  • CK Hutchison ports deal to sharpen scrutiny of TikTok US sale
  • Chinese regulators have begun looking into CK Hutchison ports
  • Beijing trying to project strong stance against US, analysts say
  • Beijing may approve TikTok deal if part of sweeping set of pacts

Beijing's criticism of Hong Kong conglomerate CK Hutchison's move to sell its ports business is a precursor to heightened political scrutiny of other major Chinese business divestments involving American buyers, analysts say.

The unexpected criticism of the deal, including the sale of assets near the Panama Canal to a BlackRock-led consortium, will, in particular, sharpen the scrutiny of a possible sale of the U.S. assets of TikTok, owned by Chinese firm ByteDance, they added.

U.S. President Donald Trump, who previously criticised what he perceived as Chinese control of the Panama Canal, hailed the deal, terming it "reclaiming" of the canal within hours of the transaction's announcement on March 4.

A week after that news, China's Hong Kong and Macau Affairs Office reposted two commentaries criticising CK Hutchison and saying the sale was a betrayal of China that neglected national interests.

Chinese regulators, under the instructions of central leadership, have begun looking into the deal, said one source with knowledge of the matter, a sign of Beijing's discontent with CK Hutchison's divestment under perceived U.S. pressure.

The person declined to be named because of the sensitivity of the matter. China's State Council Information Office (SCIO), which handles media queries on behalf of the Chinese government, did not respond to Reuters requests for comment.

CK Hutchison also did not respond. Bloomberg first reported the development on Tuesday.

The heavy politicization of the CK Hutchision deal and the sale of TikTok's U.S. business are set to cast a long shadow over deals involving Chinese and American companies amid growing tensions between the world's two largest economies.

"Beijing is balancing multiple priorities — it's trying to project a strong stance against U.S. pressure while ensuring it does not appear weak, particularly in the eyes of its domestic audience," said Patricia M. Kim, a U.S.-China relations expert and a fellow at the Brookings Institution.

Kim said the scrutiny of the CK Hutchison deal was part of Beijing's broader strategy to "adopt a more combative tone" toward the United States after unveiling countermeasures in response to trade actions by the Trump administration.

"Beijing's ultimate decision on whether to complicate the port transaction or others like the TikTok deal will likely hinge on its assessment of whether a trade deal with the Trump administration remains viable," Kim said.

Chinese officials have indicated to ByteDance executives in recent months that Beijing doesn't want to the company to be forced to sell control of the app to U.S. investors, another source with knowledge of the matter said.

The SCIO, ByteDance, and TikTok did not respond to Reuters' request for comment on the matter.

'DAMAGING IMPLICATIONS'

Beijing's unprecedented criticism of CK Hutchison's Panama withdrawal came after the deal angered President Xi Jinping, partly because the company didn't seek Beijing's approval, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing sources, on Tuesday.

Chinese leadership had planned to use the Panama port issue as a bargaining chip in negotiations with the Trump administration, only to be taken by surprise by the sale, according to the Journal.

Since Trump took office, Beijing has tried to walk a careful line, countering U.S. tariffs with its own measures while leaving the door open for talks on a bigger deal that would ease tensions.

That could include approval for asset sales such as TikTok if it was part of a more sweeping set of agreements to reset relations between the two countries, officials have said privately.

Although some analysts say China's regulatory reach over the CK Hutchison deal is limited, as none of the ports being sold are in China or Hong Kong, some legal experts say Beijing could still review the transaction.

Popular short-video app TikTok, meanwhile, facing shutdown in the United States next month if Chinese owner ByteDance does not find a U.S. buyer, must grapple with concerns about its autonomy from ByteDance and the Chinese government.

In the TikTok sale process, the White House is playing an unprecedented role - acting as an investment bank, with Vice President JD Vance running the auction. This marks a significant increase in government intervention in private business matters.

In contrast to CK Hutchison, ByteDance is heavily reliant on its home market, with business operations spanning video streaming, news aggregation, e-commerce and artificial intelligence.

Also, the Chinese government owns 1% of one of ByteDance's main subsidiaries – Beijing Douyin Information Service Ltd, through a "golden share," as explained by TikTok in a letter to U.S. senators in 2022.

The criticism of the Hutchison ports deal has made it clear if ByteDance were to accept any forced sale of TikTok without Beijing's consent, it could face not only regulatory challenges but also political resistance from Beijing, analysts said.

"There is a more realistic possibility that the ByteDance matter will eventually form part of some wider settlement," said Steve Vickers, CEO of Steve Vickers and Associates, a specialist political and corporate risk consultancy.

"Companies, large and small alike, should be mindful that this (Panama) case has severely damaging implications, which will translate into tangible business risk in Greater China, Asia and around the world," he said.

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Holds Decline
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar held its recent decline to around $0.575 on Friday amid risk-off sentiment due to lingering trade war concerns.

Investor caution persisted as U.S. President Donald Trump remained poised to implement reciprocal tariff against key trading partners on April 2.

Further weighing on the Kiwi was a stronger U.S. dollar, which strengthened after the Federal Reserve indicated it was in no rush to cut interest rates further.

Domestically, the currency failed to gain support from data showing New Zealand’s economy had climbed out of a recession, given significant pockets of weakness.

Expectations for policy easing remain firm, with markets pricing in around 60bps of rate cuts—equivalent to two or three reductions—by year-end.

Despite these headwinds, the New Zealand dollar is still heading for a modest weekly gain.
